task_completion.description: Open the FreeScout application in a web browser
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open displaying the FreeScout login page or dashboard.

task_completion.description: Log in to FreeScout using the provided admin credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeScout interface shows the user is logged in, with indicators matching the admin account (admin@helpdesk.local).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the system logs or audit logs in FreeScout
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeScout web interface displays a logs, audit, or security events page.

task_completion.description: Locate the 'Failed login' event for the admin user
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific log entry is visible on the screen indicating a 'Failed login' attempt for the admin user (admin@helpdesk.local).

task_completion.description: Open or create the target output file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is open, showing the creation or editing of a file named 'suspicious_ip.txt' in the /home/ga/ directory.

task_completion.description: Save the correct IP address to the file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'suspicious_ip.txt' is shown containing the IP address '203.0.113.88'.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeScout web interface to investigate the incident rather than bypassing it via direct database queries or raw log file reads.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active navigation and interaction within the FreeScout web GUI to find the failed login event.

integrity.description: The agent actually viewed the log entry containing the IP address before writing it to the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: At least one screenshot clearly shows the IP address '203.0.113.88' within the FreeScout logs interface before it is written to the text file.

integrity.description: The agent saved ONLY the IP address to the file, as explicitly requested in the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal command shows only the IP address ('203.0.113.88') being written to 'suspicious_ip.txt', without any additional conversational text or labels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target user email: admin@helpdesk.local. Expected output filename: suspicious_ip.txt. The correct source IP address for the failed login is 203.0.113.88.
